narrative.summary: New contemporary Italian with hidden speakeasy in back. Chef Andrew Capone. Replaced 25-yr Piri Piri Grill. 4.9 rating early. Weekend brunch + dinner.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in the Junction Triangle, on a corner that held a 25-year Portuguese grill until Andrew Capone turned it into this sleek Italian wine bar. Inside, it's all warm wood, cream-colored walls, and a bar lined with bottles that lead back to a speakeasy tucked behind the main room. The lighting is low enough to feel like evening even at brunch, and tables are spaced wide enough that you're not listening to anyone else's conversation.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Capone's cooking is contemporary Italian with real restraint—nothing overworked, nothing trying to prove itself. The branzino comes on a bed of couscous, the arancini with a tomato sauce that tastes like someone's nonna made it yesterday, and the gnocchi arrives braised with rabbit instead of the expected bolognese. His cacio e pepe is getting a reputation already. Mains run in the mid-twenties, which feels fair for cooking this confident.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Book it for a Friday or Saturday dinner when you want somewhere that feels like a find before everyone else catches on. Or go for weekend brunch with someone you actually want to talk to—the space doesn't rush you out.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Had a very enjoyable evening at their opening. Service was top notch. Had Fish with couscous, arincini, the tomatoe sauce was out standing, and a nice bottle of red. Atmosphere was very nice. Look forward to the next visit."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Perfect place to catch up with a friend and share some delicious Italian food! The branzino, cacio e pepe spaghetti, & the gnocchi with braised rabbit were to die for!! Thank you Katie, Pedro, and Tiago for the incredible service and recommendations. Will definitely be returning❣️"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The food was on point, balanced, full of flavour, and handled with a light touch that showed real restraint and confidence in the kitchen. Nothing felt overworked, and every dish tasted thoughtful and well composed. The service matched the food perfectly: attentive, warm, and present without ever feeling forced or performative.
The cocktails were spot on as well. The bartender was engaging, curious, and clearly passionate about what they’re doing. They took the time to understand our palate and seemed genuinely interested in crafting drinks that suited us, which made the whole experience feel personal and fun.
This place feels like exactly what the neighbourhood has been missing. It has an energy and sense of care behind it, and it’s the kind of spot you leave already thinking about your next visit. The tartar was my favourite dish of the night and definitely has the feel of a signature item. And the seafood linguine, made in house, was cooked perfectly, simple, elegant, and deeply satisfying.
Really looking forward to coming back. Keep up the great work Team Rossio!"
